case_id: 22-fret electric guitar, Fender 25.5" scale (648mm), Ibanez-style neck dimensions

pi_items.verified_value: Correct. All 22 fret positions match precisely (within 0.01mm) with a 648.0mm scale length using the standard equal temperament formula: fret_position = scale_length × (1 - 2^(-fret_number/12)). The 648mm scale is the standard Fender 25.5-inch scale length.
pi_items.source: Reverse-engineered from fret 22 position using fret calculation formula and verified against all 22 coordinates

pi_items.verified_value: 43.0 mm is verified as standard nut width for electric guitars. This is the standard width for Fender Stratocasters and Gibson Les Pauls.
pi_items.source: Web search results from SevenString.org, guitar neck manufacturers (Yinfente), and Warmoth confirm 43mm as standard nut width

pi_items.verified_value: 56.0 mm is verified as standard heel (body end) width for electric guitar necks. This is typical of Ibanez RG necks and modern electric guitar designs with progressive neck taper.
pi_items.source: Web search results confirm 56mm (2.20 inches) as typical heel width for electric guitar necks including Ibanez RG and other modern designs

pi_items.verified_value: 6.0 mm is valid though larger than modern standard. Modern standard fretboard dots are 2.5-3mm diameter, but 6.0-6.35mm was used on early Fender guitar inlays (clay dots ~1/4 inch = 6.35mm). 6.0mm is a valid choice for prominent inlays.
pi_items.source: Web search results from ProjectGuitar.com, StewMac, Amazon product listings, and guitar luthier forums show range of 2-7mm, with modern standard being 2.5-3mm and historical Fender using ~6.35mm

pi_items.verified_value: This is the correct standard inlay pattern for guitars up to 22 frets. Standard pattern is frets 3, 5, 7, 9, 12, 15, 17, 19, 21, 24 for 24+ fret guitars. For a 22-fret guitar, omitting fret 24 is correct. The 12th fret is the octave point where the same note repeats at higher pitch.
pi_items.source: Web search results from Guitar Gear Finder, TalkBass, guitar luthier forums confirm standard inlay pattern. Wikipedia article on guitar inlays confirms octave significance of 12th fret.

pi_items.verified_value: /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/fretboard_template.dxf is the correct output path defined in the task environment setup (verified in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh scripts)
pi_items.source: Task setup and export scripts explicitly create directory and expect file at this path

privileged_info_summary: The guitar fretboard template specifications are based on real electric guitar design standards. The 22 fret positions (36.37mm to 466.16mm) precisely match the standard equal temperament formula with a 648mm (25.5-inch) Fender scale length. The nut width of 43mm and heel width of 56mm are verified standard dimensions for modern electric guitar necks (Fender/Gibson nut, Ibanez-style heel). The 6mm inlay diameter is valid, matching early Fender clay dot inlays, though larger than modern 2.5-3mm standard. The inlay fret pattern (3, 5, 7, 9, 12, 15, 17, 19, 21) is the verified standard for guitars with 22 frets. All specifications represent real guitar design, not arbitrary values.
